mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0%, it signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, which contracts corporate revenue growth and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance with p-value greater than 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and consumer price indices indicate robust demand pulling through inflation, which forces corporate pricing power expansion and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns when RSAFS and CPIAUCSL move in opposite directions, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain efficiency, which reduces input cost pressures and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat forward returns during post-shock recovery periods, or the mechanism predictive power vanishes when GDP growth exceeds 3.0% annually.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts indicates collapsing construction demand and reduced household wealth effects, which suppresses consumer durables spending and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 5% month-over-month
failure_condition: Housing starts declines fail to predict negative equity returns when mortgage rates remain below 3.5%, or the signal out-of-sample R-squared falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates indicates labor market tightening and wage growth, which boosts corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The payroll-unemployment divergence yields no predictive power for short-term returns, or the interaction effect reverses direction when inflation exceeds 3.0% annually.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above the annualized inflation rate, real interest rates turn positive, stabilizing purchasing power and reducing equity valuation discount pressures, which produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive real rates consistently coincide with negative equity returns, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark during disinflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarterly growth in real GDP combined with expanding industrial production confirms macroeconomic momentum, which supports corporate earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP and industrial production growth fail to predict forward returns when credit spreads widen by more than 100 basis points, or the signal Sharpe ratio remains below 0.5 in walk-forward tests.
